reasoning: The FRED statistical tool gives a baseline probability of an increase of 0.30, driven heavily by mean-reversion mechanics (the current value of 6.11 represents a sharp recent spike above the baseline trend of ~5.93). A web search reveals that the FOMC is meeting on March 17-18, where a Summary of Economic Projections will be released. Recent articles cite an 'oil price shock' as a reason to expect hawkish rate signaling, which likely explains the rapid rise in the Baa yield from 5.90 to 6.11 over the past week. Because markets typically price in such expectations ahead of the actual event (buy the rumor, sell the news), a further increase post-meeting would require the FOMC to be even more aggressively hawkish than the already elevated market pricing. Therefore, keeping the provided statistical model as the primary anchor, I assess the probability of a further increase by March 22 to be 32%.
